content.text:
# Prompt 2: The check runner

Add the eval checks to /Users/sbigelow/Workspace/evals-in-ci. Two verifiers, mirroring the
essay's "two different verifiers, same lesson" — one schema check, one
property check. Keep it to two small files.

## checks/test_schema.py
Runs kubeconform against every YAML in fixtures/ with strict mode and
the resource.k8s.io/v1 schemas enabled. One pytest test, parametrized
per file. Add kubeconform to mise.toml as a tool; do not shell out to
an unpinned binary.

## checks/test_properties.py
Property checks kubeconform can't express — the "claims traceable to
source" tier. Parse fixtures with PyYAML and assert 3 properties, e.g.:
- every container sets resource requests and limits
- no image uses the :latest tag
- DRA ResourceClaimTemplate uses the GA resource.k8s.io/v1 request shape
Pick properties the vendored fixtures actually satisfy; the suite must
pass green. Add pyyaml to pyproject.

## Constraints
- pytest is the only runner. `mise run test` (or `uv run pytest`) is the
  whole interface — no wrapper scripts, no config beyond pyproject.
- Every assertion failure message must name the file and the violated
  property. A red check that doesn't say why is off-thesis.
- No helper modules, no abstractions. If a check needs a function, it
  lives in the same file.

## Commits
One commit per check file, plain messages. Confirm Entire.io captured
both sessions. Show me the passing pytest output.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Add eval checks with schema and property verifiers

1	# ADR-0008: DRA — declarative accelerator requests instead of Device Plugin
2	# extended-resource counting. GA since v1.34.
3	apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
4	kind: DeviceClass
5	metadata:
6	  name: nvidia-gpu
7	spec:
8	  selectors:
9	    - cel:
10	        expression: 'device.driver == "gpu.nvidia.com"'
11	---
12	apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
13	kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
14	metadata:
15	  name: gpu-slice-claim
16	  namespace: team-ml
17	spec:
18	  spec:
19	    devices:
20	      requests:
21	        # GA resource.k8s.io/v1: a request names a class via `exactly`
22	        # (vs `firstAvailable` for prioritized alternatives). deviceClassName
23	        # and count live under `exactly`, not directly on the request.
24	        - name: gpu
25	          exactly:
26	            deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu
27	            count: 1
28	---
29	apiVersion: v1
30	kind: Pod
31	metadata:
32	  name: inference-worker
33	  namespace: team-ml
34	spec:
35	  # Non-root, no privilege escalation, all capabilities dropped, seccomp on.
36	  # DRA injects the GPU device without needing extra pod privileges.
37	  securityContext:
38	    runAsNonRoot: true
39	    runAsUser: 1000
40	    seccompProfile:
41	      type: RuntimeDefault
42	  containers:
43	    - name: worker
44	      image: inference-worker:1.0
45	      resources:
46	        requests:
47	          cpu: "2"
48	          memory: "8Gi"
49	        limits:
50	          cpu: "4"
51	          memory: "16Gi"
52	        claims:
53	          - name: gpu
54	      securityContext:
55	        allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
56	        capabilities:
57	          drop: ["ALL"]
58	  resourceClaims:
59	    - name: gpu
60	      resourceClaimTemplateName: gpu-slice-claim
61

file.content:
# ADR-0008: DRA — declarative accelerator requests instead of Device Plugin
# extended-resource counting. GA since v1.34.
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: DeviceClass
metadata:
  name: nvidia-gpu
spec:
  selectors:
    - cel:
        expression: 'device.driver == "gpu.nvidia.com"'
---
apiVersion: resource.k8s.io/v1
kind: ResourceClaimTemplate
metadata:
  name: gpu-slice-claim
  namespace: team-ml
spec:
  spec:
    devices:
      requests:
        # GA resource.k8s.io/v1: a request names a class via `exactly`
        # (vs `firstAvailable` for prioritized alternatives). deviceClassName
        # and count live under `exactly`, not directly on the request.
        - name: gpu
          exactly:
            deviceClassName: nvidia-gpu
            count: 1
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: inference-worker
  namespace: team-ml
spec:
  # Non-root, no privilege escalation, all capabilities dropped, seccomp on.
  # DRA injects the GPU device without needing extra pod privileges.
  securityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    runAsUser: 1000
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  containers:
    - name: worker
      image: inference-worker:1.0
      resources:
        requests:
          cpu: "2"
          memory: "8Gi"
        limits:
          cpu: "4"
          memory: "16Gi"
        claims:
          - name: gpu
      securityContext:
        allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
        capabilities:
          drop: ["ALL"]
  resourceClaims:
    - name: gpu
      resourceClaimTemplateName: gpu-slice-claim

3	metadata:
4	  name: otel-collector
5	  namespace: platform-observability
6	spec:
7	  replicas: 2
8	  selector:
9	    matchLabels:
10	      app: otel-collector
11	  template:
12	    metadata:
13	      labels:
14	        app: otel-collector
15	    spec:
16	      securityContext:
17	        runAsNonRoot: true
18	        seccompProfile:
19	          type: RuntimeDefault
20	      containers:
21	        - name: otel-collector
22	          # 'contrib' build — redaction processor lives here, not in core.
23	          # Pinned to a specific release for reproducibility (bump deliberately);
24	          # never :latest in a cluster deployment.
25	          image: otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib:0.155.0
26	          args: ["--config=/etc/otel/collector.yaml"]
27	          env:
28	            - name: DEPLOY_ENV
29	              value: "prod"
30	          ports:
31	            - containerPort: 4317   # OTLP gRPC
32	            - containerPort: 4318   # OTLP HTTP
33	          resources:                 # ADR-0009
34	            requests:
35	              cpu: "250m"
36	              memory: "512Mi"
37	            limits:
38	              cpu: "1"
39	              memory: "1Gi"
40	          securityContext:
41	            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
42	            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true   # collector writes nothing to the root FS with this config
43	            capabilities:
44	              drop: ["ALL"]
45	          volumeMounts:
46	            - name: config
47	              mountPath: /etc/otel
48	              readOnly: true
49	      volumes:
50	        - name: config
51	          configMap:
52	            name: otel-collector-config
53	---

55	kind: Service
56	metadata:
57	  name: otel-collector
58	  namespace: platform-observability
59	spec:
60	  selector:
61	    app: otel-collector
62	  ports:
63	    - name: otlp-grpc
64	      port: 4317
65	    - name: otlp-http
66	      port: 4318

file.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: otel-collector
  namespace: platform-observability
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: otel-collector
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: otel-collector
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        seccompProfile:
          type: RuntimeDefault
      containers:
        - name: otel-collector
          # 'contrib' build — redaction processor lives here, not in core.
          # Pinned to a specific release for reproducibility (bump deliberately);
          # never :latest in a cluster deployment.
          image: otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib:0.155.0
          args: ["--config=/etc/otel/collector.yaml"]
          env:
            - name: DEPLOY_ENV
              value: "prod"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 4317   # OTLP gRPC
            - containerPort: 4318   # OTLP HTTP
          resources:                 # ADR-0009
            requests:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "512Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "1"
              memory: "1Gi"
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true   # collector writes nothing to the root FS with this config
            capabilities:
              drop: ["ALL"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/otel
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: otel-collector-config
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: otel-collector
  namespace: platform-observability
spec:
  selector:
    app: otel-collector
  ports:
    - name: otlp-grpc
      port: 4317
    - name: otlp-http
      port: 4318

1	# ADR-0003: Gateway API for traffic routing (replaces legacy Ingress for new work)
2	apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
3	kind: Gateway
4	metadata:
5	  name: primary-gateway
6	  namespace: platform-ingress
7	spec:
8	  gatewayClassName: envoy-gateway   # any maintained Gateway API controller
9	  listeners:
10	    - name: https
11	      protocol: HTTPS
12	      port: 443
13	      tls:
14	        mode: Terminate
15	        certificateRefs:
16	          - name: primary-gateway-tls
17	      # Default is `Same`, which would reject the tenant HTTPRoutes below (they
18	      # live in team-* namespaces, not platform-ingress). Allow routes from any
19	      # namespace carrying a `tenant` label so cross-namespace routes attach.
20	      allowedRoutes:
21	        namespaces:
22	          from: Selector
23	          selector:
24	            matchExpressions:
25	              - key: tenant
26	                operator: Exists
27	---
28	# Basic single-backend route: the default path for the host. The /payments
29	# path is owned by the weighted canary route in httproute-traffic-split.yaml —
30	# keep exactly one route per (hostname, path prefix) so Gateway API conflict
31	# resolution never has to pick a winner between them.
32	apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
33	kind: HTTPRoute
34	metadata:
35	  name: example-api-route
36	  namespace: team-payments
37	spec:
38	  parentRefs:
39	    - name: primary-gateway
40	      namespace: platform-ingress
41	  hostnames:
42	    - "api.example.internal"
43	  rules:
44	    - matches:
45	        - path:
46	            type: PathPrefix
47	            value: /
48	      backendRefs:
49	        - name: storefront-svc
50	          port: 8080
51

file.content:
# ADR-0003: Gateway API for traffic routing (replaces legacy Ingress for new work)
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: primary-gateway
  namespace: platform-ingress
spec:
  gatewayClassName: envoy-gateway   # any maintained Gateway API controller
  listeners:
    - name: https
      protocol: HTTPS
      port: 443
      tls:
        mode: Terminate
        certificateRefs:
          - name: primary-gateway-tls
      # Default is `Same`, which would reject the tenant HTTPRoutes below (they
      # live in team-* namespaces, not platform-ingress). Allow routes from any
      # namespace carrying a `tenant` label so cross-namespace routes attach.
      allowedRoutes:
        namespaces:
          from: Selector
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: tenant
                operator: Exists
---
# Basic single-backend route: the default path for the host. The /payments
# path is owned by the weighted canary route in httproute-traffic-split.yaml —
# keep exactly one route per (hostname, path prefix) so Gateway API conflict
# resolution never has to pick a winner between them.
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: HTTPRoute
metadata:
  name: example-api-route
  namespace: team-payments
spec:
  parentRefs:
    - name: primary-gateway
      namespace: platform-ingress
  hostnames:
    - "api.example.internal"
  rules:
    - matches:
        - path:
            type: PathPrefix
            value: /
      backendRefs:
        - name: storefront-svc
          port: 8080

content.content:
1	# ADR-0007: reject Pods pulling from unapproved registries. GA since v1.30.
2	apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
3	kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
4	metadata:
5	  name: restrict-image-registries
6	spec:
7	  matchConstraints:
8	    resourceRules:
9	      - apiGroups: [""]
10	        apiVersions: ["v1"]
11	        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
12	        resources: ["pods"]
13	  validations:
14	    # This policy matches resources: ["pods"] — i.e. CREATE/UPDATE of the Pod
15	    # object. It checks images on containers, initContainers, and any
16	    # ephemeralContainers already present on that object. Note: ephemeral
17	    # containers are normally injected AFTER creation via the
18	    # pods/ephemeralcontainers subresource, which this policy does NOT match,
19	    # so `kubectl debug`-style ephemeral containers are NOT gated at admission
20	    # here. scripts/validate.py flags ephemeralContainers at CI time.
21	    # The init/ephemeral lists are optional on the spec, hence the has() guards.
22	    - expression: >
23	        object.spec.containers.all(c,
24	          c.image.startsWith("registry.internal.example.com/") ||
25	          c.image.startsWith("gcr.io/distroless/")
26	        ) &&
27	        (!has(object.spec.initContainers) ||
28	          object.spec.initContainers.all(c,
29	            c.image.startsWith("registry.internal.example.com/") ||
30	            c.image.startsWith("gcr.io/distroless/")
31	          )
32	        ) &&
33	        (!has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) ||
34	          object.spec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
35	            c.image.startsWith("registry.internal.example.com/") ||
36	            c.image.startsWith("gcr.io/distroless/")
37	          )
38	        )
39	      message: "All container images (containers, initContainers, ephemeralContainers) must come from an approved registry."
40	---
41	apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
42	kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
43	metadata:
44	  name: restrict-image-registries-binding
45	spec:
46	  policyName: restrict-image-registries
47	  validationActions: [Deny]
48	  matchResources:
49	    namespaceSelector:
50	      matchExpressions:
51	        - key: tier
52	          operator: NotIn
53	          values: ["platform-system"]   # platform namespaces exempted
54

file.content:
# ADR-0007: reject Pods pulling from unapproved registries. GA since v1.30.
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  validations:
    # This policy matches resources: ["pods"] — i.e. CREATE/UPDATE of the Pod
    # object. It checks images on containers, initContainers, and any
    # ephemeralContainers already present on that object. Note: ephemeral
    # containers are normally injected AFTER creation via the
    # pods/ephemeralcontainers subresource, which this policy does NOT match,
    # so `kubectl debug`-style ephemeral containers are NOT gated at admission
    # here. scripts/validate.py flags ephemeralContainers at CI time.
    # The init/ephemeral lists are optional on the spec, hence the has() guards.
    - expression: >
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith("registry.internal.example.com/") ||
          c.image.startsWith("gcr.io/distroless/")
        ) &&
        (!has(object.spec.initContainers) ||
          object.spec.initContainers.all(c,
            c.image.startsWith("registry.internal.example.com/") ||
            c.image.startsWith("gcr.io/distroless/")
          )
        ) &&
        (!has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) ||
          object.spec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
            c.image.startsWith("registry.internal.example.com/") ||
            c.image.startsWith("gcr.io/distroless/")
          )
        )
      message: "All container images (containers, initContainers, ephemeralContainers) must come from an approved registry."
---
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries-binding
spec:
  policyName: restrict-image-registries
  validationActions: [Deny]
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchExpressions:
        - key: tier
          operator: NotIn
          values: ["platform-system"]   # platform namespaces exempted

content.text: All four fixtures validate with Kubernetes 1.34.1 schemas (which include resource.k8s.io/v1) plus the CRDs-catalog for Gateway API. Now the first check file:

input.content:
"""Schema check: every fixture must validate against its Kubernetes schema.

Runs kubeconform (pinned in mise.toml) in strict mode. Kubernetes 1.34.1
schemas cover the GA resource.k8s.io/v1 DRA types; the CRDs-catalog location
covers Gateway API, which is a CRD and absent from the core schemas.
"""

import shutil
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

FIXTURES = sorted((Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "fixtures").glob("*.yaml"))

KUBECONFORM_ARGS = [
    "-strict",
    "-kubernetes-version", "1.34.1",
    "-schema-location", "default",
    "-schema-location",
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datreeio/CRDs-catalog/main/{{.Group}}/{{.ResourceKind}}_{{.ResourceAPIVersion}}.json",
]


@pytest.mark.parametrize("fixture", FIXTURES, ids=lambda p: p.name)
def test_fixture_matches_schema(fixture: Path):
    kubeconform = shutil.which("kubeconform")
    assert kubeconform, (
        "kubeconform not on PATH — it is pinned in mise.toml; run `mise install`"
    )
    result = subprocess.run(
        [kubeconform, *KUBECONFORM_ARGS, str(fixture)],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )
    assert result.returncode == 0, (
        f"{fixture.name}: failed strict kubeconform schema validation:\n"
        f"{result.stdout}{result.stderr}"
    )

import shutil
